hook.text: When uranium decays, it doesn't just stop. It transforms through 14 different elements before becoming lead.

buildup.text: Each decay produces a new radioactive daughter element, which then decays again.

discovery.text: Uranium-238 decays through thorium, radium, radon gas, and polonium before finally becoming stable lead-206.

twist.text: Radon gas—one step in the chain—seeps into basements and is the second leading cause of lung cancer.

climax.text: Decay chains help geologists date rocks billions of years old by measuring parent-to-daughter ratios.

punchline.text: Uranium becomes lead. Fourteen steps. Billions of years.

quiz.question: What is the final stable element in uranium-238's decay chain?
